description: A salad made from edible seaweed, often dressed with ingredients such as sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and a little sweetness. It is commonly served as a chilled side dish in Japanese restaurants, though many restaurant versions are prepared products rather than traditional home-style salads.
preparation_steps: Rehydrate dried seaweed if needed, then drain well.
Toss the seaweed with the dressing and any additional vegetables.

health_advice.benefits: Can provide minerals and is usually light in calories.
health_advice.risks: Some versions can be high in sodium, and seaweed can contain high iodine levels if eaten in large amounts frequently.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Treat it as a side dish, and keep an eye on sodium and portion size if you eat seaweed often.

dietary_notes.note: Often vegan and gluten-free, but gluten-free status depends on soy sauce or other seasonings in the dressing.

allergens: Soy if soy sauce is used
